title: Melicope orbicularis

intro: Melicope orbicularis, also known as the Yellow Hibiscus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the Hawaiian Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its various health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Melicope orbicularis include flavonoids, limonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Melicope orbicularis is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical ointments and salves to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
